top_categories.workout_context: 7,874ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Schleck Gran Fondo's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before May.
At mile 60 of 98, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
80ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Schleck Gran Fondo's short-steep Ardennes rollers on asphalt forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 98 miles.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Schleck Gran Fondo. Train it until it feels boring.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 7,874ft of climbing on short-steep Ardennes rollers on asphalt does to your power file on race day.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on short-steep Ardennes rollers on asphalt. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
At Schleck Gran Fondo, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 98 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 98 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Schleck Gran Fondo, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Schleck Gran Fondo’s 98 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Schleck Gran Fondo. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Race Simulation, and Durability to prepare you for 98 miles of short-steep ardennes rollers on asphalt in Mondorf-les-Bains, Luxembourg.
